heading: Memory Requirements for Indexing Vectors
body:
MongoDB Vector Search holds the entire index in memory, so you need to ensure there's
enough memory for the MongoDB Vector Search index and JVM (Java virtual
machine) if your dataset includes full precision vectors. Each index is
a combination of the vectors being indexed and additional metadata. The
index size is primarily determined by the size of the vectors that you
are indexing, with metadata space typically being relatively nominal.

When not using quantization, MongoDB Vector Search stores the full fidelity vectors in
memory. If you enable automatic quantization, MongoDB Vector Search stores the
quantized vectors, which require significantly less resources, in memory
and the full fidelity vectors on disk. You can view the difference
between disk and memory requirements for vector indexes by viewing the
**Size** and **Required Memory** columns in the
Atlas UI MongoDB Search page.

Consider the following requirements for a **single vector**:

| Embedding Model               | Vector Dimension | Space Requirement                                  |
| ----------------------------- | ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Voyage AI voyage\_3\_large    | 2048             | 8kb (for float)2.14kb (for int8)0.334kb (for int1) |
| OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002 | 1536             | 6kb                                                |
| Google text-embedding-gecko   | 768              | 3kb                                                |
| Cohere embed-english-v3.0     | 1024             | 4kb (for float)1.07kb (for int8)0.167kb (for int1) |

 [BinData](manual/reference/method/BinData)
quantized vectors. To learn more, see
[How to Ingest Pre-Quantized Vectors](avs-bindata-vector-subtype).

The required space scales linearly with the number of vectors that you
are indexing and with the vector dimensionality. You can also use the
**Search Index Size** [MongoDB Search charts](review-atlas-search-metrics) to determine the amount of space and
memory you need on your Search Nodes.
